purpose: Standardize how retrofit DevView records a change from user intent through code anchors, implementation, evidence, and optional rollback.

target.purpose: Identify the external project and the bounded maintenance slice.

userConfirmedIntent.purpose: Record what the user actually asked for and what must not happen.

observedAnchors.purpose: Connect the intent to observed code, docs, tests, or hardware behavior.

candidateMatch.purpose: Explain why this candidate implementation path was selected.

implementationPlan.purpose: List the smallest expected source files, execution flow, and explicit non-goals.

forbiddenFlows.purpose: Prevent the most likely wrong maintenance expansion.

evidence.purpose: Separate build/test evidence from real-device or user acceptance evidence.

finalState.purpose: Say whether the external code still contains the change, whether it was reverted, and what can safely happen next.

guardrails: Do not treat inferred retrofit anchors as source authority without user confirmation.
Do not omit forbidden flows for behavior changes.
Do not merge build pass with hardware pass.
If code is reverted after validation, keep the evidence but set finalState.activeCodeState to reverted.
If external repo writes are forbidden or later reverted, record that explicitly instead of deleting the learning record.
When possible, validate reverted records against the external repo so rollback-specific signatures are absent even if the same files later change for another approved task.
